user_message: Hey, can you assess the risk for Iron Creek Mining? Board meeting is next week.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-09 10:38 EST
Subject: Re: Re: territory planning Timeline

Our team encountered some challenges with territory planning. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
Date: 2024-02-22 13:36 EST
Subject: URGENT: territory planning Issue at Iron Creek Mining

Just checking in on the territory planning proposal. Our CTO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-12 11:51 EST
Subject: URGENT: territory planning Issue at Iron Creek Mining

Our team encountered some challenges with territory planning. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

Meeting: territory planning Discussion - Iron Creek Mining
Date: 2024-02-10
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), David (Iron Creek Mining)

[00:01:30] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into territory planning.

[00:08:00] David (Iron Creek Mining): On the territory planning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:10:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Let me add some context -- we've been working on security compliance and found that there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us.

[00:13:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): On the territory planning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

[00:18:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is partner engagement.

[00:22:00] David (Iron Creek Mining): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-M trap (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-M failure mode (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct territory planning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-M -- Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
